title: Inside Active: Baillie Gifford’s Coutts, Burns Look Beyond US
summary: Europe and Japan’s earnings outlooks have improved, with double-digit growth expectations, though upgrades are concentrated in technology and energy, while inflation pressures could weigh on valuations. In this episode of Inside Active, host David Cohne, mutual fund and active management analyst at Bloomberg Intelligence, and co-host Laurent Douillet, senior equity strategist at BI, speak with Tom Coutts and Lawrence Burns, portfolio managers at Baillie Gifford, and subadvisers on the Vanguard I

title: Alphabet $80 Billion Equity Sale Set to Break Fundraising Record
summary: Alphabet Inc.’s surprise $80 billion sale of equity to fund spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure is expected to become the biggest equity capital markets transaction of all time, according to data compiled by Bloomberg.

title: Emerging-Market Currencies Gain on ‘No News Is Good News’ Day
summary: Most emerging market currencies advanced Tuesday as Iran and the US stepped back from some of the hostile rhetoric that had marked previous days, boosting risk sentiment.

title: Canadian Stocks Index Hits 35,000 Amid Rally in Energy
summary: The Canadian equities benchmark passed 35,000 for the first time on a broadening rally in energy and financials.

title: Gold Rises As Traders Weigh Confusion Over US-Iran Talks
summary: Axel Merk, founder and CIO of Merk Investments, said that while gold remains a relatively 'calm' investment, the world around the commodity and the geopolitical and economic 'crosscurrents' including AI investment and the war with Iran have created volatility in markets. 	 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Pares Gains as US Jobs Data Reinforces Higher-Rate Bets
summary: Gold pared gains after robust US jobs data reinforced bets that the Federal Reserve will keep interest rates higher for longer, a headwind for the precious metal.

title: Short-Sellers Are Dug In as Bond Market Faces Big Payrolls Test
summary: Bond traders are locked in to wagers on higher yields even after paring some of their more extreme positions, with critical US employment data due on Friday that may add to the argument for Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes.

title: Momei Qu on Berkshire Hathaway Endorsing AI
summary: Momei Qu of PSP Growth discussed the significance of Berkshire Hathaway's recent actions, interpreting them as a strong endorsement of AI as more than just market hype. According to Qu, Berkshire's involvement signals that AI represents a durable infrastructure shift that merits substantial investment. This perspective aligns with Alphabet's announcement to raise $80 billion in equity to fund its AI initiatives, underscoring the growing confidence in AI's transformative potential across industries. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Marvell Soars the Most Since 2000 After $1 Trillion Stock Call
summary: Marvell Technology Inc. shares surged the most in 26 years after Nvidia Corp.’s Jensen Huang predicted that the semiconductor and networking company would be the next business to hit a $1 trillion valuation.

title: Victoria’s Secret Stock Rises 47% | Closing Bell
summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Aberdeen Economist on Falling Brent Crude Prices
summary: Sree Kochugovindan, Senior Research Economist at Aberdeen Investments, focusing on current market sentiment. The recent decline in Brent crude prices, which dropped by 0.72% to $94.30 per barrel. The segment aims to provide insights into the factors influencing investor confidence and market dynamics in the Middle East and Africa region. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: S&P 500 Ties Longest Streak Since 1995 as AI Powers Advance
summary: The S&P 500 Index climbed for a ninth-straight session on Tuesday, matching its longest winning streak since 1995, as traders piled into artificial-intelligence stocks amid developments in the Middle East.

title: Middle Market Businesses 'Real Driver of Economy' Says Wendy Stewart
summary:
Wendy Stewart, president of Global Commercial Banking at Bank of America, said that middle market businesses are driving the economy right now, driving growth and hiring and firing amid an uncertain economic environment. 
Stewart also said that global supply chains still exist, but that businesses have to be flexible given the geopolitical landscape.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Blue Owl Tech Private Credit Fund Sells $500 Million Bonds
summary: A private credit fund managed by Blue Owl Capital Inc. sold $500 million of investment-grade bonds on Tuesday, according to a person familiar with the matter.

title: CoreWeave-Tied Data Center Raises $900 Million in Junk-Bond Sale
summary: A data center tied to CoreWeave Inc. raised $900 million from a high-yield note offering, joining a wave of junk issuers tapping debt markets to fund artificial intelligence infrastructure.

title: S&P 500 Tops 7,600 as AI Fuels Nine-Day Win Streak: Markets Wrap
summary: A revival of the artificial-intelligence trade kept driving Wall Street momentum, with stocks also rising on hopes for an agreement that would end the war that has roiled financial markets around the world.

title: UBS Sells $1.5 Billion AT1, First Since Swiss Reform Pause
summary: UBS Group AG raised $1.5 billion of Additional Tier 1 capital in US dollars, its first foray into the market since Switzerland paused plans to tighten rules on the riskiest type of bank debt.

title: Venezuela Moves to Open Power Sector to Private Investment
summary: Venezuela’s National Assembly will debate a reform that would open the country’s electricity sector to private investment, allowing companies to generate, distribute and sell power under government concessions after more than 15 years of state control.

title: Copper and Aluminum Power Higher on Global Demand, War Outlook
summary: Copper topped $14,000 a ton and aluminum advanced to its highest in more than four years as optimism over demand and supply constraints outweighed concerns over the unresolved conflict in the Middle East.

title: Cliffwater Private Credit Fund Stung by 17% Redemption Requests
summary: Cliffwater LLC’s flagship private credit fund capped redemptions at 5% in the second quarter after investors looked to pull about 17% of shares, in a sign of enduring pressure on the $1.8 trillion market.

title: Treasuries Pare Gains After Jobs Openings Back Rate-Hike View
summary: Treasuries retreated from near their best levels of the past month as the first of three labor-market reports this week backed wagers that the Federal Reserve’s next move will be to raise interest rates.

title: Oil Swings Higher as Traders Weigh Prospects of US-Iran Deal
summary: Oil edged higher as traders parsed discordant signals on the prospects of a US-Iran peace deal.

title: Traders Most Bullish Yuan in 15 Years on Global Role, Valuation
summary: Traders ramped up wagers on a stronger yuan in the coming year, betting that increased global usage and an attractive valuation will guide China’s currency through near-term speed bumps.

title: Goldman's Solomon Sees More Greed Than Fear in Markets
summary: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon says a boom in equity markets is being driven by an appetite for profit that’s outweighing fears about economic disruption and inflation risks. He speaks during an appearance at the Economic Club of New York. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Philippines' Marcos on Building a Legacy of His Own
summary: Heir to the Philippines' most famous political dynasty, President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr has had to contend with the complicated history of his family name. Now with two years left in his single-term presidency, Marcos is facing the economic consequences of a rupture in global geopolitics, a rapidly changing technological landscape, and a growing feud at home with his Vice President Sara Duterte, who is herself a successor of another prominent political family. In this latest episode of Latitude, the Philippine president opens up to Bloomberg's Haslinda Amin on how he is forging ahead to try and build a legacy of his very own.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Petrochemicals Giant Braskem Seeks Support to Start Out-of-Court Rework
summary: Braskem SA is seeking backing from creditors to begin an out-of-court restructuring process ahead of debt payments scheduled for July, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: US Natural Gas Futures Dip on Lower Flows to LNG Export Plants
summary: US natural gas futures ended lower as domestic gas flows to liquefied natural gas export terminals along the US Gulf Coast dropped for a third consecutive day. The reduction is concentrated at Sabine Pass LNG in Louisiana and Corpus Christi LNG in Texas.

title: Colombia’s Pro-Trump Presidential Frontrunner Thrills Investors
summary: Exuberant market reaction to a celebrity lawyer’s surprise victory in Colombia’s first-round election Sunday is sowing expectations for an investment rebound in the resource-rich Andean country.

title: AI Fuels $280 Billion Cybersecurity Rally as Earnings Test Looms
summary: Investors are about to get a read on the durability of the soaring rally in cybersecurity stocks when two of the industry’s major players report earnings in the coming days.

title: 12 Reasons This Is The Worst Crypto Winter Ever
summary: It’s getting colder and colder.

title: Alphabet's $80 Billion AI Power Move | Open Interest 6/2/2026
summary: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." Alphabet is raising $80 billion in equity, including an investment deal with Berkshire Hathaway to help fund its AI spending plans.  Anthropic’s IPO filing raises the stakes against OpenAI. Plus, US job openings jumped in April to the highest level in almost two years.  SpaceX negotiates to pay razor-thin fees to Wall Street firms handling its IPO. And the collector car market enters a new era. We discuss with Hagerty CEO McKeel Hagerty. And MongoDB CEO CJ Desai joins Bloomberg Open Interest to talk about how AI is powering his firm and what's next for enterprise AI. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Perplexity Is 'Chip Agnostic,' Says CEO
summary: Computex in Taiwan is putting the spotlight on the companies building the hardware behind the AI boom. One of those companies is Perplexity, which took the stage with Intel to unveil the world's first "hybrid local/server agentic inference orchestrator." Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas joins Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)
